message: Fractal Documentation System - AI-optimized hierarchical documentation
Use fractal 3-layer structure for all documentation
reasoning: Token efficiency: Strategic (800 tokens) → Tactical (3,200 tokens) → Implementation (18,000 tokens). AI can progressively load only what's needed. 97% token savings vs loading full tree.
decision: Adopt Strategic/Tactical/Implementation hierarchy for all KT branches

message: Strategic Layer - High-level architecture and decisions
content: The Strategic Layer contains high-level information: branch purpose, major decisions, architectural choices, and macro-level patterns. Typically 800-1,500 tokens. This is what AI reads first to understand if a tree is relevant.

message: Tactical Layer - Implementation approaches and modules
content: The Tactical Layer contains mid-level details: implementation strategies, module organization, component patterns, and technical approaches. Typically 2,000-5,000 tokens. AI loads this when strategic layer shows relevance.

message: Implementation Layer - Specific code and details
content: The Implementation Layer contains fine-grained details: specific files, code snippets, utilities, edge cases, and micro-optimizations. Typically 10,000-25,000 tokens. AI loads this only when coding or debugging specific features.

ISS (Index Scrolling System) integration enables automatic discovery. The hierarchical-summarizer generates 3-layer markdown summaries with YAML frontmatter. The indexer updates ISS for fast lookups. The intelligent-loader auto-discovers relevant trees based on queries.

Full tree loading: ~25,000 tokens. Strategic only: ~800 tokens (97% savings). Strategic + Tactical: ~4,000 tokens (84% savings). This enables AI to scan 30+ trees in the same token budget as loading 1 full tree. Critical for large codebases and multi-project contexts.

Use cases: (1) Planning new features - Load strategic layers of related projects. (2) Code review - Load tactical + implementation of specific modules. (3) Architecture decisions - Compare strategic layers across multiple projects. (4) Onboarding - Progressive loading from macro to micro. (5) Cross-project analysis - Scan strategic layers of entire codebase.
